research_tasks.task_title: Replot Fig. 1e, 1f, and 1i from STM/STS spectra and line profile data
research_tasks.task_description: Using the Excel file provided, reproduce the figure panels corresponding to the average tunneling spectroscopy spectra and the spatial line profile. The file contains multiple sheets with annotated columns for bias-dependent dI/dV spectra and a distance-height line scan. Your goal is to generate publication-style plots that match the figure layout: two 1D dI/dV spectra panels and one line profile panel. For the spectra, plot dI/dV versus bias with appropriate axis labels and units, preserving any multiple traces or sampling-density differences between sheets. For the line profile, plot height versus distance using the provided spatial coordinates. If the file contains multiple traces or repeated measurements, overlay them clearly or average them only if the sheet structure indicates that the figure is an average spectrum. Ensure the final output is a clean replot suitable for direct comparison with the figure in the paper.
execution_steps: Step_0: Open the workbook and identify the sheets corresponding to the spectral panels and the spatial line profile.
Step_1: Parse the annotated columns for Bias, dI/dV, Distance, and Height, checking for any unit annotations in the headers.
Step_2: Generate the dI/dV vs Bias plots for the spectral sheets, preserving trace structure and using consistent axis formatting.
Step_3: Generate the Height vs Distance line profile from the spatial sheet.
Step_4: Combine the plots into a figure layout that mirrors the target paper panels and export the result.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A replot of the Fig. 1 spectral panels and line profile, showing two bias-dependent dI/dV spectra and one distance-height line scan in a publication-style layout.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot Fig. 2b, 2c, 2d, and 2e from step-edge topography and spatial STS
research_tasks.task_description: Using the provided Excel workbook, recreate the figure panels associated with a step-edge topography profile and spatially resolved tunneling spectroscopy across two terraces. The workbook includes a topographic line profile, a multi-trace spectroscopy line scan, and representative spectra from different terrace positions. Your task is to extract the relevant columns, reshape any wide-format multi-spectrum sheet into a long or plot-ready format, and reproduce the line profile and spectra as publication-quality plots. Pay attention to the typo in the distance label and to any columns labeled by terrace position or step side. The final output should visually match the target figure panels: a step-height profile, a waterfall or stacked spectral map across the step, and comparison spectra from the two terraces, including the field comparison if present in the file.
execution_steps: Step_0: Inspect all sheets in the workbook and identify the topography profile sheet and the spectroscopy sheets.
Step_1: Standardize the x-axis labels, correcting any spelling inconsistencies such as 'Disntance' to 'Distance' for plotting purposes.
Step_2: Plot the topographic line profile as Height versus Distance with correct units.
Step_3: Reshape the multi-trace spectroscopy sheet into a format suitable for plotting a waterfall or stacked line scan across the step.
Step_4: Plot the representative dI/dV spectra for the two terrace regions and, if available, the magnetic-field comparison spectra.
Step_5: Assemble the panels into a figure layout corresponding to the target paper figure and export the result.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A replot of the step-edge figure showing the topographic height profile, spatially resolved spectroscopy across the step, and terrace-comparison dI/dV spectra.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot Fig. 3b, 3c, 3d, and 3e from layer-dependent STM/STS data
research_tasks.task_description: Using the Excel workbook, reproduce the figure panels that compare topography and tunneling spectra across multiple FeSn layers. The file contains a height profile across several unit-cell steps, a multi-spectrum dataset organized by layer number, and a summary plot of spectral peak height versus layer. Your task is to extract the layer-resolved spectra, identify the layer labels, and generate a clean replot of the topographic line scan and the layer-dependent spectral comparisons. If the workbook includes a separate summary sheet for peak height versus layer or field, plot it directly using the provided values. The final result should reproduce the figure panels that show alternating behavior across layers and the corresponding quantitative trend plots.
execution_steps: Step_0: Open the workbook and locate the sheets for the topographic line profile, layer-resolved spectra, and summary trend plots.
Step_1: Parse the layer labels and associated dI/dV traces, ensuring each spectrum is assigned to the correct layer number.
Step_2: Plot the height profile as Height versus Distance for the multi-step terrace scan.
Step_3: Plot the layer-resolved dI/dV spectra versus Bias, using consistent styling to distinguish layers.
Step_4: Plot the summary metric versus layer number if a dedicated sheet is present, preserving the original axis scaling and labels.
Step_5: Arrange the plots into a figure layout matching the target paper figure and export the final replot.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A replot of the layer-dependent figure with a terrace height profile, layer-resolved spectra, and peak-height trend plots versus layer number.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot Fig. 4b, 4c, and 4d from site-resolved STM/STS on inequivalent atomic sites
research_tasks.task_description: Using the provided Excel workbook, recreate the figure panels that compare topography and site-resolved tunneling spectra on inequivalent atomic sites of the FeSn surface. The workbook includes a line profile across the atomic lattice and separate dI/dV spectra for distinct site labels such as Sn1, Sn2, Kagome1, and Kagome2. Your task is to extract and plot the topographic profile, then generate the site-resolved spectra as publication-style line plots. If the workbook contains paired spectra sheets, plot them side by side or in the same panel with a legend so that the site-to-site contrast is clear. The final output should match the figure panels showing atomic-site-dependent electronic structure.
execution_steps: Step_0: Inspect the workbook and identify the topography sheet and the site-resolved spectroscopy sheets.
Step_1: Parse the Distance and Height columns for the topographic line scan and plot the profile.
Step_2: Parse the Bias and dI/dV columns for each site label, confirming which traces correspond to Sn1, Sn2, Kagome1, and Kagome2.
Step_3: Plot the site-resolved spectra with clear legends and consistent axis formatting.
Step_4: If the workbook includes multiple spectra per site, overlay them or separate them according to the sheet structure.
Step_5: Combine the plots into a figure layout that mirrors the target paper figure and export the result.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A replot of the site-resolved figure showing the topographic line profile and dI/dV spectra for inequivalent atomic sites.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot Fig. 5e and 5f from magnetic-field-dependent bound-state spectroscopy
research_tasks.task_description: Using the Excel workbook, reproduce the figure panels that show the evolution of tunneling spectra with magnetic field and the fitted peak position versus field. The file contains a field series of dI/dV spectra and a summary table with peak position and uncertainty values as a function of B. Your task is to plot the spectra for each magnetic field value using the provided Bias and dI/dV columns, then plot the extracted peak position versus magnetic field with error bars if the uncertainty column is present. Preserve the field ordering and use the exact field labels from the workbook. The final output should be a clean replot of the field-dependent spectroscopy and the quantitative peak-tracking panel.
execution_steps: Step_0: Open the workbook and identify the field-series spectroscopy sheet and the peak-tracking summary sheet.
Step_1: Parse the Bias axis and the corresponding dI/dV traces for each magnetic field value.
Step_2: Plot the full set of spectra as a field-dependent series, using a consistent color map or line ordering keyed to B.
Step_3: Extract the peak position and error values from the summary sheet and plot peak position versus B with error bars.
Step_4: Verify that the field labels are preserved exactly as listed in the workbook and that the axis units are clearly shown.
Step_5: Assemble the two panels into a publication-style figure and export the result.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A replot of the magnetic-field-dependent spectra and the peak position versus field summary, including uncertainty if available.
